title: Supporting Afghan nationals: Special measures for Afghan applications - Canada.ca
content:
## Special measures for Afghan applications
Due to the situation in Afghanistan, special measures were put in place for certain types of applications to:
- prioritize processing under regular immigration programs for sponsoring certain family members (family reunification) permanent residence as a protected person in Canada permanent residence as a dependant of a resettled Afghan refugee temporary residence
- sponsoring certain family members (family reunification)
- permanent residence as a protected person in Canada
- permanent residence as a dependant of a resettled Afghan refugee
- waive fees and/or certain requirements for citizenship documents, passports, and travel documents for Canadian citizens and permanent residents Afghan nationals abroad who are approved for permanent residence in Canada temporary residence applications for Afghan nationals accompanying their immediate family members who are Canadian citizens or permanent residents in Canada
- citizenship documents, passports, and travel documents for Canadian citizens and permanent residents Afghan nationals abroad who are approved for permanent residence in Canada
- temporary residence applications for Afghan nationals accompanying their immediate family members who are Canadian citizens or permanent residents in Canada
- accompanying their immediate family members who are Canadian citizens or permanent residents
These special measures ended on December 31, 2023 and are no longer in effect.

## If you have a matter pending before the Immigration and Refugee Board of Canada
Find out how the Immigration and Refugee Board of Canada is processing existing files for Afghan nationals .
